questions.text: The Mughals were descendants of two great lineages of rulers: Genghis Khan and:
questions.explanation: From their mother's side they were descendants of Genghis Khan and from father's side, Timur.

questions.text: Who was the first Mughal Emperor?
questions.explanation: Babur succeeded to the throne of Ferghana in 1494 and captured Delhi in 1526.

questions.text: In which battle did Babur defeat Ibrahim Lodi in 1526?
questions.explanation: The first Battle of Panipat established the Mughal rule in India.

questions.text: Babur used ________ effectively in the first battle of Panipat.
questions.explanation: Babur was the first to use artillery extensively in the Indian subcontinent.

questions.text: Which Mughal Emperor was forced to flee to Iran after being defeated by Sher Shah Suri?
questions.explanation: Sher Khan defeated Humayun at Chausa (1339) and Kanauj (1340).

questions.text: Akbar became Emperor at the young age of:
questions.explanation: Akbar's reign can be divided into three periods, starting from 1556.

questions.text: Who was Akbar's regent during his early years of rule?
questions.explanation: Bairam Khan managed the empire until Akbar became independent in 1560.

questions.text: Jahangir’s name before becoming Emperor was:
questions.explanation: Prince Salim took the title Jahangir (Conqueror of the World).

questions.text: The Mughal Emperor who built the Taj Mahal in memory of his wife was:
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan's reign is known for architectural brilliance.

questions.text: Which Mughal Emperor faced the maximum rebellions from Marathas and Sikhs in the later part of his reign?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb's long reign in the Deccan led to exhaustion of the empire's resources.

questions.text: The Mughal system of assigning ranks to officers was called:
questions.explanation: Mansab means a position or rank, determining status and salary.

questions.text: In the Mansabdari system, 'Zat' determined the:
questions.explanation: The higher the zat, the more prestigious was the noble's position.

questions.text: The 'Sawar' rank in the Mansabdari system determined the:
options: Number of cavalrymen to be maintained
questions.explanation: The sawar rank indicated the military strength expected from the noble.

questions.text: Mansabdars received their salaries as revenue assignments called:
questions.explanation: Jagirs were somewhat like iqtas, but most mansabdars did not actually reside in or administer them.

questions.text: The revenue system developed by Todar Mal was called:
questions.explanation: Zabt involved the careful survey of crop yields, prices, and areas cultivated for a 10-year period.

questions.text: Who was Akbar's revenue minister?
questions.explanation: Todar Mal carried out a careful survey of agriculture for ten years (1570-1580).

questions.text: The history of Akbar’s reign, 'Akbar Nama', was written by:
questions.explanation: The third volume of Akbar Nama is called 'Ain-i Akbari'.

questions.text: Akbar divided his empire into provinces called:
questions.explanation: Each suba was governed by a Subadar who exercised both political and military functions.

questions.text: The financial officer of a Mughal province was called a:
questions.explanation: The Diwan maintained the accounts and revenue collection of the suba.

questions.text: Akbar's idea of universal peace and tolerance was called:
questions.explanation: Sulh-i-kul focused on ethics, honesty, and peace that was applicable across all religions.

questions.text: The 'Ibadat Khana' was a building built by Akbar for:
questions.explanation: At Fatehpur Sikri, Akbar discussed religion with Ulama, Brahmanas, Jesuit priests, and Zoroastrians.

questions.text: Who was the most influential queen of Jahangir?
questions.explanation: Mehrunnisa was given the title Nur Jahan and exercised significant political power.

questions.text: The Mughals did not like to be called 'Mughal' or 'Mongol' because of:
options: The memory of Genghis Khan’s massacre of people
questions.explanation: Genghis Khan's name was associated with massacres, whereas they were proud of their Timurid ancestry.

questions.text: The town where Akbar shifted his capital in 1571 was:
questions.explanation: Fatehpur Sikri served as the Mughal capital for a brief period.

questions.text: In the Mughal army, the 'Bakshi' was the:
questions.explanation: The Bakshi was responsible for the payment and administration of the army.

questions.text: A 'Zamindar' in the Mughal context was a:
options: Village headman or local chieftain acting as an intermediary
questions.explanation: Zamindar was the term used to describe all intermediaries who collected revenue from peasants.

questions.text: Which religious community did the Sikhs form during the 17th century?
questions.explanation: The Khalsa was a militarized community established to resist Mughal authority.

questions.text: Aurangzeb was given the title of:
questions.explanation: Alamgir means 'Conqueror of the World'.

questions.text: The practice of 'primogeniture' means:
options: Eldest son inherits the father's estate
questions.explanation: The Mughals did not follow primogeniture, instead preferring 'coparcenary' inheritance (division among all sons).

questions.text: Babur defeated Rana Sanga at:
questions.explanation: The victory at Khanua in 1527 consolidated Babur's position in North India.

questions.text: Which of these was the mother of Shah Jahan?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan's mother was a Rathor princess from Jodhpur.

questions.text: Akbar's army defeated the forces of Hemu in 1556 at:
questions.explanation: The second battle of Panipat secured Akbar's reign.

questions.text: The 'Sadr' in Mughal administration was the minister in charge of:
questions.explanation: The Sadr advised the Emperor on religious grants and donations.

questions.text: The main source of income for Mughal rulers was tax on:
questions.explanation: Land revenue was the backbone of the Mughal economy.

questions.text: Which monument is located in the Red Fort complex, Delhi, built by Shah Jahan?
questions.explanation: The Diwan-i-Aam was where the Emperor met the general public.

questions.text: Mughal painting reached its zenith during the reign of:
questions.explanation: Jahangir was a great patron of art and an expert judge of paintings.

questions.text: Which Sikh Guru was executed by Aurangzeb?
questions.explanation: The execution of Guru Tegh Bahadur in 1675 led to increased Sikh resistance.

questions.text: The Maratha leader who escaped from Aurangzeb's prison and declared himself an independent king was:
questions.explanation: Shivaji's escape from Agra in 1666 was a major blow to Aurangzeb's prestige.

questions.text: Why did the Mughal Empire face a crisis of jagirs towards the end of the 17th century?
options: Shortage of jagirs and increase in the number of mansabdars
questions.explanation: The number of mansabdars increased drastically, leading to a long wait for a jagir and corruption.

questions.text: The term 'Faujdar' in the Mughal administration refers to a:
questions.explanation: Faujdars were responsible for maintaining peace and order in their districts.
